text.title: yaml-frontmatter Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for yaml-frontmatter as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tab characters used for indentation inside frontmatter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: YAML strictly requires spaces. A tab character causes a parse error or silently corrupts the block in lenient parsers.

failure-mode.description: Comma-separated string used where an array is required: `tags: a, b, c`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This parses as a scalar string, not an array. Use `tags: [a, b, c]` or multiline YAML array syntax.

failure-mode.description: Patching only one field with an agent regenerates the entire frontmatter block, erasing `created` and other agent-set fields.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents asked to "update the status field" often rewrite the full block. Use `yq` for single-field patches to preserve all other fields.

failure-mode.description: Nesting frontmatter more than 2 levels deep.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deeply nested frontmatter is difficult to query with standard tools and breaks most SSG schema validators. Move complex data to the document body.
